scope_note: QA Fibonacci Depth Decomposition cert [419]. Primary sources: Lucas, E. (1878) 'Theorie des fonctions numeriques simplement periodiques' American Journal of Mathematics 1(2) pp. 184-240 (Fibonacci doubling identity F_{2n}=F_n*L_n and odd identity F_{2n-1}=F_n^2+F_{n-1}^2; foundational Lucas sequence theory); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p) and Pisano period); Lehmer, D.H. (1930) 'An extended theory of Lucas functions' doi:10.2307/1968235 (Lucas divisibility theory including L_{alpha/2} divisibility). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The Fibonacci depth invariant delta(p) = F_{alpha(p)} / p mod p (cert [417], well-defined since p|F_{alpha(p)}) decomposes according to the parity of alpha(p): (EVEN alpha) delta(p) = F_{alpha/2} * (L_{alpha/2}/p) mod p, where p ALWAYS divides L_{alpha/2} (algebraic corollary: F_{2n}=F_n*L_n with p|F_{2n} and p notdivides F_{alpha/2} forces p|L_{alpha/2}); (ODD alpha) delta(p) = (F_k^2 + F_{k-1}^2) / p mod p where k=(alpha+1)/2, from the identity F_{2k-1}=F_k^2+F_{k-1}^2. (C1) EVEN-ALPHA DECOMPOSITION: verified for all 62 even-alpha primes in [2,500]; Lucas divisibility p|L_{alpha/2} confirmed (0 failures); decomposed delta matches direct delta for all 62 primes; falsifiable: any prime with even alpha(p) where the decomposed delta disagrees with direct delta refutes; (C2) ODD-ALPHA DECOMPOSITION: verified for all 33 odd-alpha primes in [2,500]; identity F_{2k-1}=F_k^2+F_{k-1}^2 mod p^2 confirmed; decomposed delta matches direct delta for all 33; (C3) FIBONACCI PRIME SUBCLASS: for Fibonacci primes p=F_m (p in {2,3,5,13,89,233,1597} = all Fibonacci primes <=2000): alpha(p)=m and F_{alpha(p)}=p exactly (F_m mod p^2 = p since F_m=p<p^2); therefore delta(p) = p/p mod p = 1; verified for all 7; mechanism: F_{alpha}=p is exact (not just mod p^2); (C4) FULL DELTA CENSUS: delta=1 primes up to 2000 are exactly {2,3,5,13,41,89,193,233,1597,1621}; verified by computing delta(p) for all 303 primes <=2000; non-Fibonacci delta=1 primes are {41,193,1621}; each satisfies 2*alpha(p)=p-(5/p) (maximum rank condition from cert [416]): p=41 alpha=20 2*20=40=41-1=p-(5/p=+1); p=193 alpha=97 2*97=194=193+1=p-(5/p=-1); p=1621 alpha=810 2*810=1620=1621-1=p-(5/p=+1); falsifiable: any prime <=2000 with delta=1 not in the listed set, or any listed prime with delta!=1, refutes; the deeper algebraic characterization of non-Fibonacci delta=1 primes beyond the maximum-rank condition remains open (Wall-Sun-Sun connection: the maximum-rank primes are those where phi/phi-bar has order (p-(5/p))/2 in F_{p^2}^times, a half-generator condition). CHAIN: [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p) -> [417] alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p), delta(p) defined -> [418] alpha parity via Cassini -> [419] delta decomposition by parity; lucas doubling identity (Lucas 1878) + Cassini (1680) meet the QA T-step orbit. All integer arithmetic, no float, Theorem NT. Derived 2026-06-14.
